summary.intent: Investigate whether Telegram forum topic isolation is fully implemented in moltis, then fix any bugs found and ship a PR.
summary.outcome: Two bugs were identified and fixed in crates/chat/src/lib.rs: (1) thread_id added to ChannelReplyTargetKey to prevent stream dedup collisions across topics, and (2) all ~30 outbound call sites updated to use target.outbound_to() instead of target.chat_id so replies actually land in the correct forum topic thread.

repo: Session isolation by topic is keyed on (channel_type, account_id, chat_id, thread_id) — correctly implemented upstream in gateway/channel_events.rs.
The composite 'chat_id:thread_id' format used for addressing is only produced by outbound_to() on ChannelReplyTarget — raw chat_id and thread_id are stored as separate fields.

parse_chat_target() in crates/telegram/src/outbound.rs handles splitting the composite address on every outbound method (send_text, send_html, send_media, etc.), so passing the composite string is the correct calling convention.
ChannelReplyTargetKey is only ever constructed via From<&ChannelReplyTarget>, never instantiated directly — tests are safe from struct field changes.
A pre-existing cmake build issue causes clippy on the full workspace to fail; per-crate clippy (moltis-chat) is clean.

code.finding: ChannelReplyTargetKey was missing thread_id, causing stream dedup to treat all topics in a supergroup as the same target — could silently drop text delivery for a topic if another topic in the same supergroup had already streamed.
send_stream in ChannelStreamDispatcher passed &target.chat_id instead of &target.outbound_to(), routing streaming edits to the top-level chat rather than the forum topic.

All outbound calls in deliver_channel_replies_to_targets, deliver_channel_error, send_logbook_follow_up_to_channels, send_retry_status_to_channels, and media/document/location dispatch functions used &target.chat_id instead of &target.outbound_to() — ~30 call sites total.
Every outbound method calls parse_chat_target(to) — the composite 'chat_id:thread_id' string is the expected input format.

workflow: Bind let to = target.outbound_to().into_owned() before spawning tasks so the borrow is resolved before the closure captures target.
Tracing macros (warn!, debug!) logging chat_id = target.chat_id are correct to leave unchanged — they log the raw ID for observability.

friction: Pre-existing cmake build issue causes full-workspace clippy to fail, obscuring whether lints are clean — had to fall back to per-crate clippy.
~30 call sites needed individual fixes with no bulk-replace safe pattern (logging macros interspersed with outbound calls use the same target.chat_id expression).
open_items: Verify the fix in a live multi-topic supergroup environment — unit tests pass but the routing behavior wasn't integration-tested against real Telegram.
Consider adding a lint or type-level wrapper to prevent future call sites from accidentally passing raw chat_id where outbound_to() is required.
